topic: A person finds their spouse's secret savings account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The envelope was tucked behind a stack of old tax documents, something I hadn't even bothered to look through in years. My hand trembled slightly as I pulled it out. Inside was a bank statement, and the numbers staring back at me made my breath catch in my throat. It wasn't just a deposit; it was a substantial sum, meticulously saved over several years, hidden away. I reread the figures three times, the ink blurring slightly under my intense scrutiny. A cold knot tightened in my stomach, pulling my shoulders up toward my ears. I slowly lowered the paper, my gaze darting to the closed door of the study, suddenly feeling exposed in the quiet room.

[story 2]

I found the access code scribbled on the back of a receipt for a rare book. My fingers fumbled with the keypad, my heart hammering a frantic rhythm against my ribs. When the screen finally illuminated, displaying a balance that made my vision swim, I physically recoiled, stumbling back against the bookshelf. A strange dryness coated my mouth. I stared at the screen, unable to process the sheer magnitude of the money. My jaw went slack, and I could feel a sudden, dizzying lightness in my chest, as if the air had been sucked out of the room. I looked up, expecting to see my spouse, but the silence of the apartment felt deafening.

[story 3]

It was a misplaced folder in the filing cabinet that led me to the truth. When I finally opened the ledger, the figures swam before my eyes. A sudden wave of nausea washed over me, making my hands slick. I leaned closer, needing to confirm what my eyes were registering. The total was astronomical. A low, involuntary sound escaped my throat—a choked whisper that barely registered above the hum of the refrigerator. I backed away slowly, my eyes wide and unfocused, taking in the reality of the situation as if it were a distant, unbelievable landscape. My whole body felt rigid, every muscle tense with an unfamiliar, profound stillness.
